chunks.video_title: Installing VS Code & How Websites Work
chunks.text: From today's video, we will start the Sigma Web Development course. Like it with high energy, because in this video, I am going to make you a profitable web developer from scratch. This course will be very modern because web development has changed over time. Many new things have come, many such things have come in JavaScript, which makes the work very easy. Many such technologies have come that will make your work very easy. This course will be for those who know nothing, if you know absolutely zero about web development, this course is for you. We will start from zero, but further, we will raise the bar like anything. What all we will learn in this course, I will tell you shortly. Web development is a very profitable thing. I have personally earned a lot of money through web development. And my belief is that you can also be quite successful by learning web development. During the course, you will be given notes, cheat sheets, references, all these things. This course is going to be a bit different because in this course, according to my experience, I will tell you all the technologies that you should learn, as much as you should learn. And I will make this course a complete course with all the web development technologies. The course content will be complete web development, front-end, back-end, database, I will teach you as much as you should learn. What exactly the course content will be, I will tell you later, but trust me that I will make you a full-stack developer. Further, you will be given many projects during the course, and at the end of the course, we will create some good projects so that your grip on web development becomes very strong. According to me, project-based learning is the best way to learn. That's why I will create many projects for you, and if you agree, then write 'I agree' in the comment section below. Because when you comment, a smile comes on my face, so please do comment. I want to assure you that after completing this course, you will be able to create any type of website from start to finish. And during this course, I will also make you build many such websites so that your confidence will go very high. So let's start with the first video of our Sigma Web Development course. Let's roll the intro.

chunks.video_title: Installing VS Code & How Websites Work
chunks.text: Now I want to tell you how websites work. A website basically works through three types of people. Imagine three people. One is Client brother, one is Server brother. Who is it? One is Client brother, who is the other? Server brother. Who is the third one? Browser brother. Who are also lovingly called Babu Bhaiya. Browser brother, okay. So Browser brother, Client brother, Server brother, what is this client, what is this server, what is this browser? I am the client, you, all of us who use a browser. Who is the browser? This is Chrome. Hello Chrome. I am the client. I will tell the client, when I type codewithharry.com, let's say I type codewithharry.com. So what do I basically say? I tell this browser, 'Please give me the codewithharry.com website'. Or if I type youtube.com here, I say, 'Give me the youtube.com website'. I say, 'Browser sir, please give me youtube.com, I want to watch YouTube'. What does the browser say? 'You want YouTube? Here you go, okay'. So here, if I type youtube.com and press Enter, it fetches youtube.com from the server. Are you understanding? It fetched youtube.com from the server, parsed it, and showed me this screen. This work happened so quickly because my internet.com is very... not internet.com, my internet speed is very fast. Even if it's less, nowadays people's speeds are even faster. But still, according to my locality, it's very fast, okay. So with this fast internet speed, all these things are happening very fast, okay. So the client sends a request to the server through the browser. The browser is the client's, meaning my assistant. Whatever I tell the browser, it will do that, it will help me fetch this webpage from the server. Who is managing the server? YouTube people are managing the server, they are managing youtube.com's server. And inside that server, some code is written that sends me this kind of code. That means, when I type youtube.com, this code came. Now, how to convert this code into this, that's the browser's job. That means, how to display it to me, that's the browser's job. So the client says something like this to the browser, 'Hey, what's up, give me youtube.com'. The browser says, 'What's up, you want youtube.com? Wait, let me ask youtube.com for youtube.com'. Then the browser goes to the server, meaning it sends a request to the server, and tells the server, 'Give me youtube.com'. The server will either give it to him, or say it's not there, or say 'error', or say that the server error occurred, it wasn't found. Whatever the response is, ideally it should be 200, there's a response code 200, with that response, our browser comes back to the client and displays this kind of page. I hope this is clear to you. Client, browser, server, okay. So client to browser, browser to server, then browser back to client. So this is how it works. This thing is a bit more important, I'll explain it one more time. When you type google.com, any website, google.com. So the client tells the browser, 'Bring me google.com'. The browser says, 'Hey Gurudev, you want google.com? Wait, I'll fetch it from the server right now'. So the browser goes to the server, fetches google.com, and gives it to you, and you get google.com. Now, along with google.com, the server sends all the images, all the videos, whatever things there are, like these icons, it sends all of them. The URLs of these icons are also written in this code. And it's also the browser's job to display them correctly to us. So the server sends a complete bundle to the browser, it sends this kind of bundle, and it's the browser's job to parse this bundle and display it in this way. Now, whatever further interaction the user does with this page, the browser will make additional requests. If I right-click and inspect, and then go to network, and here I type, say, 'Harry bhai', okay, let's say I typed 'Harry bhai'. So look, it has sent so many requests for images and many other requests. So as you interact, more additional requests will have to be sent to the browser. So all in all, you can understand this like a restaurant, you placed an order. That is, you sent a request as soon as you placed the order. A waiter took your order, that waiter then went into the kitchen, he sent your order to the kitchen, you can consider the kitchen as the server. And after that, finally, when the kitchen gave the order back, what did this waiter, who is acting as the browser, do? That waiter gave you the order back. It's possible that the order couldn't be made in the kitchen for some reason. It's very rare that you order something and don't get it, but it happens sometimes. Similarly, here too, sometimes you encounter errors like 404 error or 500 error, server not working, these kinds of errors. So whenever such errors are encountered, you are informed, but ideally, a 200 (OK) response comes, and you get this kind of webpage. So you can also understand this like a restaurant. So whenever you type google.com/mail, if google.com/mail is available, it will show you. And if you type codewithharry.com/contact, if codewithharry.com/contact is available, it will show you; if not available, it will show 404, or if there's a 500 error, it will show you a 500 page. Whatever the server sends, it will show you. So this cycle keeps going on, the client sent a request. The browser then sent it to the server, the server then processed the request, after that the server sent the data back to the browser, and whatever data was sent, the browser showed it to the client. Then the client did something, the browser sent the same request to the server, then the server sent something, whatever data the server sent came back to the client through the browser, and so on.

chunks.video_title: Installing VS Code & How Websites Work
chunks.text: So now you'll say, 'Okay, all this is fine, but what is backend, what does backend mean?' Backend means how the server is processing the request. Now, let's say you have a blog, and there's a lot of data inside the blog. So what will you do? Will you store each webpage of the blog separately, or will you dynamically generate that content? You will dynamically generate that content. I'll take Google's example. If I search for 'Harry bhai' here. So you can see here that Google's header, these links, this format doesn't change at all. If I write, say, Shubham here, okay, let's say I wrote Shubham here. So as soon as I type Shubham here, you see that when I typed Harry bhai, Google is showing me very similar content. So what Google does is, Google's server, its backend, processes the data in such a way that it can send this kind of HTML for the corresponding query. That is, when I write Shubham, it sends this. When I write Harry bhai, it sends this. So the process of generating this thing, through which system it is happening, that is the backend. Now this backend can be Django. This backend can be Flask. This backend can be Node.js. This backend can be PHP. This backend can be Ruby on Rails. And the database inside the backend is used. That means, the backend code is not visible to the client. This is the front-end code that the server sends. And how the server created this code, we don't know. Because this code is running on the server. If you are getting confused, don't get confused at all, you will understand all these things. We will look at the backend later. For now, let's understand how the frontend works.

chunks.video_title: Installing VS Code & How Websites Work
chunks.topic: Introduction to Frontend Languages (HTML, CSS, JavaScript)
chunks.text: Now, the frontend is primarily made up of three languages. HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. These are three languages that are absolutely essential if you are building a website. Every website needs HTML, CSS, JavaScript in most of the cases. If you only use HTML, then a very basic skeleton of the website is created. After that, when you use CSS, you can style it, for example, this button looks like this because of CSS. Along with that, this button here looks red, this button here looks somewhat curvy, all this is happening with the help of CSS. The edges you see here, all this is happening with the help of CSS. So CSS's job is to style a page. What color a button will have, what will happen on hover, all these tasks are done through CSS. In a way, CSS is the beauty of an HTML page. HTML is the existence, the body of any page. Understand it like this: this is a standard that has been agreed upon. Anyone who builds a website will build it with HTML so that browser developers can understand how to parse it. The browser's job is to parse HTML, and HTML is the skeleton. This is a standard. Anyone who builds a website in this world will build it in HTML, using HTML as the skeleton. This is a standard. Such a standard has been created so that everyone creates the same kind of pages, and if someone is building a browser, they have to parse the same language. If as a beginner you have learned 80% HTML, then you are good to go for web development. Now, some things can be done directly with HTML. For example, if I show you a very quick HTML code. So I'm writing HTML code like this, by typing an exclamation mark, I've used Emmet abbreviation here. Don't worry at all about how I wrote this code. I will write 'Hey, this is HTML' and I can directly open this HTML file like this. And as soon as I open this HTML file directly here, you'll see it opens like this. I can open it directly, I can actually double-click here like this. And it's open now, look, 'Hey, this is HTML' is written. But it's not styled at all, meaning this HTML file is very basic. Now, with HTML, you can directly play videos. I have an HTML file that I'll show you, with which you can play videos. I'll do one thing, I'll show you by changing this HTML file such that we can display videos in it. So in HTML, there's a video tag. If you type 'video' and press Enter, whatever video you put inside 'src' will play. For example, if I put video1.mp4 here. You see, VS Code automatically showed me video1.mp4. This is the advantage of using VS Code. So you can see here that I will reload this, and this video automatically appeared here, I can play it. To play it, I will have to write 'controls' here. And if I refresh it now, I will get the play controls, I can play it. Man, this place is missing, the guy desperately needs money to sell, he needs cash. He's asking for 130 crore, final, absolutely. Now if I put video2.mp4 here. I had put video one here, outside I have two more videos. I have some other videos here, if I put this here and then refer to them, if I put video2.mp4 instead, then video two will appear. I'll show you by reloading. And why did you put credit? So, put Ben Stokes and show people by working hard with your own hands, move forward. These were Ben Stokes' look-alikes. Now if I make it video three here, let's say I make it video three here. Then my third video will play. So I will reload it, and you guys can see, I can play it. Hey sir, give me something to eat, sir. This third video of ours played, I played video3.mp4 here. Hey sir, give me something to eat, sir. So I will close this now, and similarly, we can also play video four. And video four is a DJ video I have, in which a DJ plays. So look at this. Jai Bholenath, all of you, father, father. So you can create such things using only HTML. That means you don't need to do anything else. Here, with just HTML, you can play videos, display images, all this can be done with just HTML.

chunks.video_title: Installing VS Code & How Websites Work
chunks.topic: Roles of CSS and JavaScript in Web Development
chunks.text: Now, if you want to style it, for example, let's say I want a border on it, or I want it to have a red background. So what will I have to do for that? For that, I will have to use CSS, and CSS adds beauty to any website. Then comes JavaScript, which adds interactions and so on to a website for you. For example, what will happen when I click on this search? What will happen when I type 'Code with Harry' here? For example, when I typed 'Code with HR', many auto-complete suggestions appeared. Why did they appear? They appeared because a request went to the server, and for this query, auto-complete results appeared here. So all these things are done with JavaScript. This is what we call client-side scripting, meaning if we want to do scripting, coding, right here on the client side, we can do that with the help of JavaScript. It's also the brain, so our website becomes beauty with brain. I'm talking about the website, beauty with brain. Beauty was added with CSS, and the brain came from JavaScript. Now, JavaScript easily helps you make the page dynamic. For example, if you want to change content without reloading the page, all this is done with JavaScript. There are a lot of mosquitoes here, so let me just kill these mosquitoes. Oh, these are big mosquitoes, nothing is happening to them here. My racket is working. Oh my God. Like the video because I also take electric shocks for you.

chunks.video_title: Installing VS Code & How Websites Work
chunks.topic: Web Development Learning Strategy & Course Approach
chunks.text: So when I was in college, someone enlightened me that I should learn HTML, CSS, JavaScript one by one. First, learn 100% HTML, then learn 100% CSS until you remember all the properties, then learn JavaScript. But this notion is absolutely wrong. Why is it wrong? Because you can start web development by learning only 80% HTML, only 40-60% CSS, and only 50-70% JavaScript. You can create projects, and by continuously creating, you can move forward from there. Okay, further in this course, I will also tell you about ChatGPT and its impact on web development. So we will discuss this too. You guys stay tuned. So never chase perfection with HTML and CSS. Don't think, 'Oh, I'll do HTML for one year, then CSS next year, then JavaScript in the third year, then build projects in the fourth year.' Never think like that. Do HTML 80%, CSS 40-60%, and by learning 50-70% of JavaScript, you can create a very good website. And as you keep building websites, your experience will teach you more HTML, more CSS, and more JavaScript, and you won't even realize that you have perfected HTML, CSS, and JavaScript while you are making money and working as a professional web developer. So you will be learning as well as earning, and along with that, you will also be advancing your skills. So never think that I will only build a website when I remember all HTML tags, or when I remember all CSS properties, or when I know all JavaScript. I will teach you only what matters, you have to learn what matters. And I don't want you to keep learning for years, that's why I have designed this course in such a way so that you are only taught what matters. And you will learn the rest of the things through practice, and I am going to give you a lot of practice in this course itself. Here, a couple of mosquitoes have also come to learn the Sigma Web Development course, which is good, our audience is growing. In this course, we will first learn HTML, then CSS, then JavaScript. And in this course, I will tell you such tricks that you would not have heard anywhere else, and I have learned those tricks at different points in my career, I will tell you right here in one course. I learned some amazing tricks quite late in my career, and I want you to learn all those tricks right from the start. Now, if you have any doubt in this course or in the upcoming videos, you can ask your doubt using #doubt, using this hashtag. I am going to try to answer all the doubts, I will try my best to solve your doubts. So if you want to ask any technical question, please use #doubt. And please, don't use #doubt in irrelevant questions or comments. Write them without #doubt so that I can prioritize replying to comments with #doubt. I will try to answer all the doubts as much as I can, and you will learn everything in this course. That's why, like this course if you haven't already. That will bring a smile on my face, so please do it, man.

So the skeleton is HTML. After that, if skin and everything else is added, it's CSS. And if the brain is added, meaning your nervous system, that's JavaScript.

Just like any other website. Now if I right-click here and click on inspect, you see, the inspect element has opened here. Now here, you can see that inside the head, they have used script, and to add CSS here, they have used link. So what I will do here is remove their CSS from here. So to remove CSS, I will simply clear all their style tags using the delete key. So as soon as I remove all these style tags, and along with that, all these link tags. So as soon as I deleted all their stylesheets, look at the condition of this page. This page looks something like this, meaning it only has raw HTML. What should be the size of images, what kind of images should be there, it has forgotten all this. And only raw HTML is written here, which has not been styled at all, so forbes.com's condition has become something like this without CSS. That means, without CSS, it's showing raw buttons like this, raw text like this, raw links like this. So this is the role of HTML, CSS, and JavaScript in creating a webpage.

chunks.video_title: Installing VS Code & How Websites Work
chunks.topic: Course Conclusion, Earning Potential & Next Steps
chunks.text: For your information, I want to tell you that this course was initially made to be paid, but I changed my mind and converted it into a free course, and I thought, 'Man, I'll put it on YouTube,' so I want all of you to be serious. Many people also reached out to me to launch this course as a paid course, many big companies. But a voice from my heart was saying that this course should be kept free because when I used to learn web development, I can understand when you don't have a good course to learn from and you don't have the affordability to buy a paid course, so I can understand, and I decided that this Sigma Web Development course should be launched absolutely free of cost. This course is free, but under this free plan, as you earn crores of rupees in the coming time, you are going to give me tea, coffee, parties, and all gifts. This is our agreement in this video.
